ontology.name: Professional Services Contract & Statement of Work Ontology
ontology.description: Models consulting agreements, contractor contracts, and statements of work (SOW) used to formalize professional service engagements between a service provider company and a client or consultant, covering services, compensation, IP rights, and legal terms.

nodes.label: Agreement / Contract
nodes.description: The top-level legal document establishing a professional services relationship between parties
nodes.semantics: Varies by subtype: Consulting Agreement, Statement of Work, Company Address Registration Agreement, or Contract. Each has a unique identifier/number.

nodes.label: Agreement Identifier
nodes.description: Unique alphanumeric identifier or number assigned to the agreement
nodes.semantics: Varies per document; often encodes client or project code and date

nodes.label: Agreement Type
nodes.description: The category of service contract document
nodes.semantics: Key axis of variation determining structure, sections, and required fields

nodes.label: Effective Date
nodes.description: The date on which the agreement becomes legally binding and effective
nodes.semantics: Varies per instance; may differ from execution/signing date

nodes.label: Service Provider / Executor / Company
nodes.description: The entity providing professional services; typically the company issuing the contract
nodes.semantics: Usually Westline Doo Beograd acting as executor or company; role label varies by document type

nodes.label: Client / Consultant / Contractor
nodes.description: The counterparty receiving or providing services depending on agreement direction
nodes.semantics: Can be an individual consultant, a foreign company, or an individual entrepreneur; role varies

nodes.label: Party Legal Name
nodes.description: Full legal name of the party as registered
nodes.semantics: Varies per instance; can be individual name or company name

nodes.label: Party Address
nodes.description: Physical or registered address of the party
nodes.semantics: Varies; includes street, city, state, zip, country
examples: 150 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA

nodes.label: Registration / Tax ID Number
nodes.description: Official company registration number, VAT/TAX ID, or employer identification number
nodes.semantics: Present for companies; varies by jurisdiction

nodes.label: Party Contact Information
nodes.description: Contact details including phone number and email address for notices
nodes.semantics: Varies per party; email used for formal notices

nodes.label: Authorized Representative / Signatory
nodes.description: The individual who signs on behalf of a party, with their name and title
nodes.semantics: Each party has one; name and title vary per instance

nodes.label: Services Description
nodes.description: The defined scope of professional services the consultant or contractor will perform
nodes.semantics: Varies widely; can be a list, table with modules/tasks, or narrative description

nodes.label: Service Domain / Type
nodes.description: The category or domain of services being performed
nodes.semantics: Key differentiator; drives specificity of deliverables and technical requirements

nodes.label: Deliverable / Milestone
nodes.description: A specific output or milestone to be produced and delivered under the agreement
nodes.semantics: Varies per project; often presented in a table with due dates and costs

nodes.label: Deliverable Due Date
nodes.description: The date by which a specific deliverable must be completed and submitted
nodes.semantics: Varies per milestone; specified in milestone table

nodes.label: Deliverable Cost
nodes.description: The payment amount associated with a specific deliverable or milestone
nodes.semantics: Varies per milestone; currency may be USD or EUR

nodes.label: Contract Term / Duration
nodes.description: The time period during which the agreement is in effect
nodes.semantics: Expressed as start and end dates or a duration; varies per agreement

nodes.label: Start Date
nodes.description: The date on which services commence under the agreement
nodes.semantics: Often same as effective date; varies per instance

nodes.label: End Date
nodes.description: The date on which the agreement or services conclude
nodes.semantics: Varies per instance; may be fixed date or event-based

nodes.label: Auto-Renewal Clause
nodes.description: Whether the agreement automatically renews at the end of the term
nodes.semantics: Boolean; consulting agreements often explicitly state no auto-renewal
examples: Agreement will not be automatically renewed
renewed periodically for additional 6 or 12 months

nodes.label: Fee / Compensation Structure
nodes.description: The model by which the service provider is compensated
nodes.semantics: Key dimension: hourly rate vs fixed fee drives payment schedule and tracking

nodes.label: Hourly Rate
nodes.description: The per-hour compensation rate when fee structure is hourly
nodes.semantics: Varies per agreement; currency in USD, EUR, or RSD

nodes.label: Total / Fixed Fee
nodes.description: The total aggregate payment amount for the entire scope of work
nodes.semantics: Present in fixed-fee agreements and aggregate caps in hourly agreements

nodes.label: Payment Schedule / Terms
nodes.description: How and when payments are made: monthly, quarterly, upon milestone acceptance
nodes.semantics: Varies; monthly arrears common for hourly, milestone-based for fixed-fee
examples: payable monthly in arrears 30 days after receipt of invoice

nodes.label: Intellectual Property Ownership
nodes.description: Clause specifying who owns inventions, work product, and IP created under the agreement
nodes.semantics: Typically company/client owns all IP; varies in scope of rights assigned
examples: Company shall own solely and exclusively in perpetuity all right title and interest
Client shall own all right title and interest in source code

nodes.label: Confidentiality / Non-Disclosure
nodes.description: Obligations to keep agreement terms and proprietary information confidential
nodes.semantics: Standard clause; scope and exceptions vary slightly
examples: All terms must be kept confidential unless disclosure required by law
Consultant will hold in confidence and not disclose Proprietary Information

nodes.label: Non-Solicitation Clause
nodes.description: Restriction on soliciting or hiring the other party's employees during and after the agreement
nodes.semantics: Present in detailed consulting agreements; typically 1-year post-service period
examples: Consultant will not encourage or solicit any employee of Company for 1 year after services

nodes.label: Representations and Warranties
nodes.description: Consultant's representations about qualifications, originality of work, and compliance with laws
nodes.semantics: Standard in detailed agreements; covers professional standards, IP non-infringement, compliance
examples: Services will be performed in professional workmanlike manner
Consultant has necessary qualifications and experience

nodes.label: Termination Provisions
nodes.description: Conditions and procedures under which the agreement may be terminated by either party
nodes.semantics: Varies in notice periods and grounds; typically 5-30 days written notice
examples: Company may terminate upon 5 days written notice
Consultant may terminate after 30 days if Company fails to pay
Client may terminate if not satisfied with service quality

nodes.label: Governing Law / Jurisdiction
nodes.description: The legal jurisdiction whose laws govern interpretation and enforcement of the agreement
nodes.semantics: Varies; Serbia most common, also California or state of incorporation
examples: laws of the State of California

nodes.label: Dispute Resolution Mechanism
nodes.description: Process for resolving disputes, typically arbitration with specific rules and venue
nodes.semantics: Varies between simple governing law clause and detailed JAMS arbitration provisions
examples: arbitration in State of California per JAMS rules

nodes.label: Exclusivity Clause
nodes.description: Whether the agreement is exclusive or permits parties to work with others
nodes.semantics: Many agreements explicitly state non-exclusive arrangement
examples: Agreement is not exclusive and Parties entitled to enter other similar agreements
Consultant may be engaged in other business not conflicting with Company

nodes.label: Independent Contractor Status
nodes.description: Clause establishing consultant/contractor as independent business, not employee
nodes.semantics: Standard in most agreements; affects tax, benefits, and liability
examples: Consultant is providing services as independent business
each party shall be and act as independent contractor and not a partner

nodes.label: Amendments Provision
nodes.description: How changes to the agreement must be made and documented
nodes.semantics: Always requires written form signed by both parties
examples: amendments must be in writing signed by both Parties
changes determined by Annex to the Contract signed by both parties

nodes.label: Severability Clause
nodes.description: Provision that invalid or unenforceable clauses do not invalidate the whole agreement
nodes.semantics: Standard boilerplate; near-identical across documents
examples: if any provision found void, remaining provisions remain enforceable

nodes.label: Entire Agreement / Integration Clause
nodes.description: Clause stating the document supersedes all prior agreements on the subject matter
nodes.semantics: Standard; confirms document is complete and final
examples: constitutes entire agreement superseding all prior agreements and understandings

nodes.label: Statement of Work (SOW) / Exhibit A
nodes.description: Attached exhibit defining specific services, term, and fees for a given work order under a master agreement
nodes.semantics: Can be standalone or attached to master consulting agreement; numbered sequentially

nodes.label: SOW Number / Reference
nodes.description: Sequential number identifying the statement of work within a master agreement
nodes.semantics: Increments per engagement; referenced in agreement header

nodes.label: Context and Background Section
nodes.description: Narrative description of the business context and purpose of the project
nodes.semantics: Present in SOW-type documents; describes business problem being solved
examples: Platform connecting neighborhood retailers with last-mile couriers
Android logistics application for neighborhood retailers

nodes.label: Assumptions, Risks and Constraints
nodes.description: List of project assumptions, out-of-scope items, and risk management procedures
nodes.semantics: Present in detailed SOWs; if violated triggers change request process
examples: Payments system integration is out of scope
Executor responsible for choosing technical stack

nodes.label: Acceptance of Deliverables
nodes.description: Process by which client evaluates and accepts or rejects completed deliverables
nodes.semantics: Present in project-based SOWs; specifies re-submission timeframe
examples: Client determines in sole discretion if deliverables meet acceptance criteria; executor must remedy failures within 5 days

nodes.label: Communication Plan
nodes.description: Agreed meeting cadence and communication protocols between parties during project execution
nodes.semantics: Present in larger project SOWs; varies in frequency and format
examples: Weekly meeting, bi-weekly demo session, on-demand meetings for requirement clarification

nodes.label: Weekly Hours Cap
nodes.description: Maximum number of billable hours per week for hourly engagements
nodes.semantics: Present in hourly agreements; typically 40 hours/week

nodes.label: Currency
nodes.description: The monetary currency used for compensation in the agreement
nodes.semantics: Varies by party geography and negotiation; affects amount interpretation

nodes.label: Indemnification Clause
nodes.description: Obligation of consultant to defend and hold harmless the company from claims by consultant's personnel
nodes.semantics: Standard in detailed consulting agreements
examples: Consultant agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company from claims by Consultant's personnel for compensation, tax, insurance

nodes.label: Assignability
nodes.description: Whether and how each party may assign their rights and obligations under the agreement
nodes.semantics: Typically company can assign freely; consultant/contractor cannot without consent
examples: Consultant will not assign without written consent of Company
Company may fully assign and transfer this Agreement

pipeline.promptReady:
# Professional Services Contract & Statement of Work Ontology

Models consulting agreements, contractor contracts, and statements of work (SOW) used to formalize professional service engagements between a service provider company and a client or consultant, covering services, compensation, IP rights, and legal terms.

**Domain:** legal

## Classes

### Agreement / Contract
The top-level legal document establishing a professional services relationship between parties

**Properties:**
- Agreement Type: The category of service contract document
  Examples: Consulting Agreement, Statement of Work (SOW), Contractor Contract, Company Address Registration Agreement
- Agreement Identifier: Unique alphanumeric identifier or number assigned to the agreement
  Examples: NORTH4082023, AM100320251, BL1601241, SV0801231
- Effective Date: The date on which the agreement becomes legally binding and effective
  Examples: 01.08.2023, 07/17/2023, 24.06.2024
- Fee / Compensation Structure: The model by which the service provider is compensated
  Examples: Hourly Fee of $30 per hour, Fixed fee of $20,000, Hourly fee of 45.00 EUR, Monthly package 60 EUR VAT included
- Governing Law / Jurisdiction: The legal jurisdiction whose laws govern interpretation and enforcement of the agreement
  Examples: laws of Serbia, laws of the State of California, laws of Republic of Serbia
- Exclusivity Clause: Whether the agreement is exclusive or permits parties to work with others
  Examples: Agreement is not exclusive and Parties entitled to enter other similar agreements, Consultant may be engaged in other business not conflicting with Company
- Independent Contractor Status: Clause establishing consultant/contractor as independent business, not employee
  Examples: Consultant is providing services as independent business, each party shall be and act as independent contractor and not a partner
- Amendments Provision: How changes to the agreement must be made and documented
  Examples: amendments must be in writing signed by both Parties, changes determined by Annex to the Contract signed by both parties
- Severability Clause: Provision that invalid or unenforceable clauses do not invalidate the whole agreement
  Examples: if any provision found void, remaining provisions remain enforceable
- Entire Agreement / Integration Clause: Clause stating the document supersedes all prior agreements on the subject matter
  Examples: constitutes entire agreement superseding all prior agreements and understandings
- Assignability: Whether and how each party may assign their rights and obligations under the agreement
  Examples: Consultant will not assign without written consent of Company, Company may fully assign and transfer this Agreement

**Relations:**
- has party → Service Provider / Executor / Company
- has party → Client / Consultant / Contractor
- defines services → Services Description
- valid for → Contract Term / Duration
- governs IP via → Intellectual Property Ownership
- includes → Confidentiality / Non-Disclosure
- includes → Non-Solicitation Clause
- includes → Representations and Warranties
- terminated per → Termination Provisions
- disputes resolved by → Dispute Resolution Mechanism
- implemented through → Statement of Work (SOW) / Exhibit A
- includes → Indemnification Clause

**Examples:**
- Consulting Agreement
- Statement of Work No NORTH4082023
- Contract AM100320251
- Company Address Registration Agreement

### Service Provider / Executor / Company
The entity providing professional services; typically the company issuing the contract

**Properties:**
- Party Legal Name: Full legal name of the party as registered
  Examples: Westline Doo Beograd, Brightline Labs Inc, Aslan Demirci, Cohort Office d.o.o
- Party Address: Physical or registered address of the party
  Examples: Knez Mihailova 12, 11000 Belgrade, Serbia, 150 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
- Registration / Tax ID Number: Official company registration number, VAT/TAX ID, or employer identification number
  Examples: 20435189, 107284316, 87-4129055, 20518734
- Party Contact Information: Contact details including phone number and email address for notices
  Examples: contact@westlinedoo.com, MPETROVIC@WESTLINEDOO.COM, +381 645 217 904
- Authorized Representative / Signatory: The individual who signs on behalf of a party, with their name and title
  Examples: Marko Petrović, CEO, Daniel Whitcomb, Chief Operating Officer, Helena Park, CEO/Cofounder

**Examples:**
- Westline Doo Beograd
- Cohort Office d.o.o

### Client / Consultant / Contractor
The counterparty receiving or providing services depending on agreement direction

**Properties:**
- Party Legal Name: Full legal name of the party as registered
  Examples: Westline Doo Beograd, Brightline Labs Inc, Aslan Demirci, Cohort Office d.o.o
- Party Address: Physical or registered address of the party
  Examples: Knez Mihailova 12, 11000 Belgrade, Serbia, 150 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
- Party Contact Information: Contact details including phone number and email address for notices
  Examples: contact@westlinedoo.com, MPETROVIC@WESTLINEDOO.COM, +381 645 217 904
- Authorized Representative / Signatory: The individual who signs on behalf of a party, with their name and title
  Examples: Marko Petrović, CEO, Daniel Whitcomb, Chief Operating Officer, Helena Park, CEO/Cofounder
- Registration / Tax ID Number: Official company registration number, VAT/TAX ID, or employer identification number
  Examples: 20435189, 107284316, 87-4129055, 20518734

**Examples:**
- Mirela Ardelean
- Northcrest AI, Inc.
- Brightline Labs Inc
- Alessio Marconi
- Selvedge AI Inc

### Authorized Representative / Signatory
The individual who signs on behalf of a party, with their name and title

**Examples:**
- Marko Petrović, CEO
- Daniel Whitcomb, Chief Operating Officer
- Helena Park, CEO/Cofounder

### Services Description
The defined scope of professional services the consultant or contractor will perform

**Relations:**
- produces → Deliverable / Milestone

**Examples:**
- AlgorithmsforAuto-Labeling, GroundTruthDataModeling, MLProgramming
- machine learning software engineering services
- Android logistics application development

### Deliverable / Milestone
A specific output or milestone to be produced and delivered under the agreement

**Properties:**
- Deliverable Due Date: The date by which a specific deliverable must be completed and submitted
  Examples: 08/17/2023, 10/17/2023, 12/17/2023
- Deliverable Cost: The payment amount associated with a specific deliverable or milestone
  Examples: $4,000, $1,000, 1000 EUR, $500

**Examples:**
- Orders API
- Tracking on Map
- Youtube Video Scraper Library
- Background removal module

### Contract Term / Duration
The time period during which the agreement is in effect

**Properties:**
- Start Date: The date on which services commence under the agreement
  Examples: 01.08.2023, August 1, 2023, January 16, 2024
- End Date: The date on which the agreement or services conclude
  Examples: 31 October 2023, Jan 30, 2024, March 27, 2026
- Auto-Renewal Clause: Whether the agreement automatically renews at the end of the term
  Examples: Agreement will not be automatically renewed, renewed periodically for additional 6 or 12 months

**Examples:**
- 01.08.2023 to 31 October 2023
- 6 months starting August 1, 2023 ending Jan 30, 2024
- 12 months

### Intellectual Property Ownership
Clause specifying who owns inventions, work product, and IP created under the agreement

**Relations:**
- assigned from → Service Provider / Executor / Company
- vests in → Client / Consultant / Contractor

**Examples:**
- Company shall own solely and exclusively in perpetuity all right title and interest
- Client shall own all right title and interest in source code

### Confidentiality / Non-Disclosure
Obligations to keep agreement terms and proprietary information confidential

**Examples:**
- All terms must be kept confidential unless disclosure required by law
- Consultant will hold in confidence and not disclose Proprietary Information

### Non-Solicitation Clause
Restriction on soliciting or hiring the other party's employees during and after the agreement

**Examples:**
- Consultant will not encourage or solicit any employee of Company for 1 year after services

### Representations and Warranties
Consultant's representations about qualifications, originality of work, and compliance with laws

**Examples:**
- Services will be performed in professional workmanlike manner
- Consultant has necessary qualifications and experience

### Termination Provisions
Conditions and procedures under which the agreement may be terminated by either party

**Examples:**
- Company may terminate upon 5 days written notice
- Consultant may terminate after 30 days if Company fails to pay
- Client may terminate if not satisfied with service quality

### Dispute Resolution Mechanism
Process for resolving disputes, typically arbitration with specific rules and venue

**Relations:**
- applies → Governing Law / Jurisdiction

**Examples:**
- Commercial Court in Belgrade
- arbitration in State of California per JAMS rules
- relevant Serbian arbitration institution

### Statement of Work (SOW) / Exhibit A
Attached exhibit defining specific services, term, and fees for a given work order under a master agreement

**Properties:**
- SOW Number / Reference: Sequential number identifying the statement of work within a master agreement
  Examples: #1, #2, #7

**Relations:**
- defines → Services Description
- valid for → Contract Term / Duration
- compensated via → Fee / Compensation Structure
- contextualized by → Context and Background Section
- constrained by → Assumptions, Risks and Constraints
- accepted per → Acceptance of Deliverables
- managed via → Communication Plan

**Examples:**
- Statement of Work #1
- Statement of Work #7
- Exhibit A - Statement of Work #2

### Context and Background Section
Narrative description of the business context and purpose of the project

**Examples:**
- Platform connecting neighborhood retailers with last-mile couriers
- Android logistics application for neighborhood retailers

### Assumptions, Risks and Constraints
List of project assumptions, out-of-scope items, and risk management procedures

**Examples:**
- Payments system integration is out of scope
- Executor responsible for choosing technical stack

### Acceptance of Deliverables
Process by which client evaluates and accepts or rejects completed deliverables

**Examples:**
- Client determines in sole discretion if deliverables meet acceptance criteria; executor must remedy failures within 5 days

### Communication Plan
Agreed meeting cadence and communication protocols between parties during project execution

**Examples:**
- Weekly meeting, bi-weekly demo session, on-demand meetings for requirement clarification

### Indemnification Clause
Obligation of consultant to defend and hold harmless the company from claims by consultant's personnel

**Examples:**
- Consultant agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company from claims by Consultant's personnel for compensation, tax, insurance

## Dimensions

### Agreement Type
The category of service contract document

### Service Domain / Type
The category or domain of services being performed

### Fee / Compensation Structure
The model by which the service provider is compensated
**Values:** Hourly Rate, Total / Fixed Fee, Currency

### Currency
The monetary currency used for compensation in the agreement
